message.content: Cloud Storage Object Discovery involves adversaries enumerating objects within cloud storage infrastructure to gather information for subsequent actions. This technique is akin to File and Directory Discovery on local systems but is applied to cloud environments. Adversaries may leverage APIs provided by cloud service providers, such as ListObjectsV2 in AWS and List Blobs in Azure, to list and access stored objects. Tools like Peirates can facilitate the listing of AWS S3 buckets. The potential impact includes unauthorized access to sensitive data, leading to data breaches or further exploitation. Common adversary tactics include automated discovery and enumeration of storage services to identify valuable data. Mitigation strategies involve implementing strict access controls, monitoring API usage for unusual activity, and employing encryption to protect data at rest. Regular audits and adherence to the principle of least privilege can further reduce the risk of exploitation.
